title: Kobe Bell - Wikipedia
content: The Kobe Bell (also known as the Friendship Bell) is a ceremonial Japanese bonshō (bell) installed on the grounds of the Seattle Center in Seattle, Washington,

title: The Kobe Bell Meditation Garden - Clio
content: The Kobe Bell Meditation Garden at the Seattle Center represents friendship between the international sister cities of Seattle, Washington, and Kobe, Japan. The cast bronze bell was created for the 1962 World’s Fair and was presented to Seattle as a gift from the people of Kobe. The Kobe Bell at the 1962 World's Fair in Seattle. The Kobe Bell chimes during a 2015 ceremony in Seattle to commemorate 20 years since the Great Hanshin Earthquake, which occurred in Kobe, Japan, in 1995. This cylindrical, cast bronze bell was sent from Kobe, Japan, to its sister city of Seattle, as a hallmark of friendship in advance of the 1962 World's Fair held in Seattle. A few years later, the city of Kobe sent a Friendship Bell to Seattle as a gift that could be dedicated and displayed during the 1962 World's Fair held in Seattle.

title: Kobe Friendship Bell - Seattle, WA, USA - Waymarking
content: The Kobe Friendship Bell is located in a small temple pagoda near the Intiman Playhouse on the Seattle Center grounds in Seattle, Washington

title: Kobe Bell in Seattle, WA - Public Art Archive
content: This familiar feature in the Seattle Center is a symbol of the friendly relationship between Seattle and our sister city Kobe, Japan.

title: Kobe Bell – Works - Office of Arts & Culture
content: The cylindrical Kobe Bell is a tribute to the goodwill and friendship fostered by Seattle's sister-city partnership with Kobe, Japan.
